notes:
Refinery cannot run tests: (1) Molecule default test_command='go test ./...' is wrong for Python/Flask project. (2) pytest not installed in PATH. (3) PostgreSQL not available locally. Rig needs test_command configured and local test environment documented. Discovered during merge of polecat/obsidian/vi-3aq@mmdu5kek — proceeded as change was .gitignore only.
Fixed by adding scripts/run_tests.sh — discovers venv (project-local or ~/vitalitek/.venv), sets DATABASE_URL to Docker port 5438, and calls pytest. Refinery command: ./scripts/run_tests.sh
title: vitalitek rig missing test environment config for refinery

description:
attached_molecule: vi-wisp-obm0
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-05T19:05:34Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Run the full Ghost Security scan pipeline on this rig's codebase. Execute these three skills in sequence:

1. Invoke /ghost-repo-context — maps the repo structure, detects projects, writes architecture summary to ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/cache/repo.md
2. Invoke /ghost-scan-code — runs AI-powered SAST scan using the context from step 1; covers SQLi, XSS, SSRF, BOLA, path traversal, and other OWASP vectors
3. Invoke /ghost-report — aggregates all scan findings into a prioritized report at ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/scans/<sha>/report.md

After the report is generated, read it and post a summary to the bead notes including: total findings count, high/medium/low breakdown, and top 3 most critical issues found.
design:
## Ghost Security Scan Report — vitalitek (4ec5850)

### Summary
- Scans: Code (SAST) — quick depth
- Vectors: authz/bola, injection/sql-injection, authn/broken-authn
- Files analyzed: 9 across 3 vectors
- Findings: 1 verified medium, 0 high/critical, 0 rejected

### Finding: OAuth callback bypasses account deactivation check
- Severity: Medium
- CWE: CWE-287 (Improper Authentication)
- Location: src/auth.py:316 (callback function)
- Issue: Google OAuth callback grants session to deactivated users because it does not check user.is_active. Local login at line 136 correctly denies deactivated accounts.
- Impact: Admin account deactivation is ineffective for OAuth users.
- Fix: Add is_active check after user lookup in OAuth callback, redirect to login with error flash if deactivated.

### Areas with No Findings
- BOLA (authorization bypass): All examined routes (superbill_routes, automation_routes, user_routes) properly filter by organization_id.
- SQL injection: All examined code (code_autocomplete_service, dashboard_service, admin_routes) uses SQLAlchemy ORM parameterized queries.

### Full report
~/.ghost/repos/vitalitek-d10530cb/scans/4ec5850/report.md
notes: Security scan complete (commit 4ec5850). Total findings: 1. Breakdown: 0 high, 1 medium, 0 low. Top finding: OAuth callback bypasses account deactivation check (Medium, CWE-287) at src/auth.py:316 — deactivated users can regain access via Google OAuth because the callback does not check user.is_active, while the local login path does. Remediation: add is_active check in OAuth callback matching local login pattern. Scans run: code (SAST) — 3 vectors (BOLA, SQLi, broken-authn), 9 files analyzed. No critical/high findings. No dependency or secrets scans were run.
title: Security scan: vitalitek

Implement Phase 8.5F from roadmap.md: Testing & Launch.

Goal: Full E2E validation, compliance review, soft launch with pilot medspas.

- Test email deliverability (open rate target: 60%+)
- Test all Stripe flows: success, failure, refund
- Security review for HIPAA compliance
- Legal review: terms of service, patient disclosures
- Soft launch with 1-2 pilot medspas
- First revenue generated!

Success Criteria:
- Email open rate: 60%+
- Patient opt-in rate: 25%+
- Insurance approval rate: 70%+
- Payment success rate: 95%+
- Average fee: $16-24 (2% of $800-1,200 typical reimbursement)

Revenue target at scale:
- 10 medspas × 20 superbills/month × 25% opt-in = 50 submissions
- 50 × $20 fee × 70% approval = $700/month
- 100 medspas = $7,000/month = $84,000/year

Estimated: 1 week. Depends on Phase 8.5E (vi-wsi).
notes: Phase 8.5F complete: 98 new tests (6 test files) covering EDI 837P, payment service, submission service, clearinghouse factory, patient portal, and E2E flow. HIPAA security hardening: encrypted PHI columns (EncryptedString), submission_token validation with constant-time comparison, PHI removed from URL params (uses Flask session), PHI removed from logs, error responses sanitized. Migration 017 adds submission_token column.

- Implement clinic selection step
- Implement client selection/creation
- Implement line item entry with autocomplete
- Add real-time calculation display
- Implement finalize workflow
- Write unit tests (90%+ coverage)
- Test end-to-end superbill creation

Success Criteria:
- Users can create superbills through UI

notes:
Analysis complete. Key issues found:
1. CSRF token missing from fetch calls - API will reject all JS requests
2. Code search JS checks data.success/data.data.codes but API returns {codes: [], count: N}  
3. Client creation not wired - builder sends client object but API needs client_id
4. Line items not posted after superbill creation - just sent in payload but ignored
5. Missing ICD-10 diagnosis codes per line item (required for finalization)
6. Date of service hardcoded to today
7. saveDraft/finalize check data.success but API doesn't return success key
8. superbill_list uses inner join on Client but client_id is nullable (migration 013)

Plan: Fix builder.html JS (CSRF, response format, multi-step save flow, diagnosis codes, date picker per line item), fix user_routes.py outerjoin, fix superbill_routes.py nullable client handling.
title: Phase 8.1: Core Superbill Creation UI

description:
attached_molecule: vi-wisp-z1m7
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-05T19:28:58Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Security fix from ghost-scan-code. CWE-287.

In src/auth.py line 316, the Google OAuth callback grants sessions to deactivated users.
The local login at line 136 correctly checks user.is_active but the OAuth path skips it.

Fix: after finding an existing user by google_sub, add the is_active check before updating:

    if user:
        if not user.is_active:
            flash('Your account has been deactivated. Please contact an administrator.', 'error')
            return redirect(url_for('auth.login'))
        # ... rest of existing update logic

Also check if any other OAuth providers (if they exist) have the same missing check.
Add a test that verifies a deactivated user cannot log in via OAuth.
title: Fix: OAuth callback missing is_active check (vitalitek)

description:
attached_molecule: mol-refinery-patrol
attached_at: 2026-03-04T04:25:29Z

Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to their effective target branches one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (quality review, if enabled)      │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
| judgment_enabled | false | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
| review_depth | standard | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |

## Target Resolution Rule

When instructions reference `<rebase-target>`, `<merge-target>`, or `<verification-target>`,
resolve that placeholder with this rule:

- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true": use MR target when present, otherwise main.
- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": always use main.

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.
title: mol-refinery-patrol
